betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6ICF Components & Laser System§f

§aBuilding and optimizing the ICF laser assembly for maximum power output!§f

The ICF laser is the initiator of fusion — composed of multiple blocks around the reactor chamber. Lasers cannot be reflected or redirected and must directly face the chamber input.

§6Laser Components§f:
All prefixed with "ICF" in-game.

§b• ICF Laser Controller§f (hbm:icf_controller) — designates direction of laser output. Must face the reactor chamber.
§b• ICF Laser Cells§f — placed in a line behind the controller. Extend as far as desired.
§b• ICF Flash Bulbs§f — adjacent to cells, require adjacent capacitors
§b• ICF Capacitors§f (hbm:icf_laser_component:4) — adjacent to flash bulbs
§b• ICF Turbochargers§f — adjacent to capacitors, REQUIRED for operation
§b• ICF Casing§f (hbm:icf_component:3) — encases all components (like PWR casing)
§b• ICF Ports§f — replace casing blocks, provide power and redstone control

§6Assembly§f:
§b1.§f Build interior: Controller → Cells → Flash Bulbs → Capacitors → Turbochargers
§b2.§f Encase all components in ICF Casing (must be airtight, like PWR)
§b3.§f Add ports for power input
§b4.§f Right-click controller to assemble
§b5.§f If no errors, casing blocks show a gray border = successful assembly

§6Power Formula§f:
Laser power consumption (HE/s) is calculated based on capacitor and turbocharger count.
Divide by §e20§f for HE/t.

§6Stellar Flux Production§f:
Q = E × 10^-11 (Q = mB/t, E = TU)
At max heat, §e1 mB/t§f stellar flux requires §e101.6 M TU/t§f heat.

§eTip§f: The ideal laser design balances capacitor count against turbocharger count for your target power level. Start conservative and expand as needed. The ICF casing must be complete — any gaps will prevent assembly!
